title: cicuta africana
intro: Cicuta africana, also known as water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to South Africa. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Cicuta africana include its potential use as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, although its toxicity means it must be handled and prepared with extreme caution. Herbal preparations of Cicuta africana are typically made as infusions or decoctions, often in combination with other plants, and should only be administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
